intro.uz: Birinchi dars — usulning oʻzi haqida. AJ Hoge kuchli holatda (peak state) tinglashni, ovoz chiqarib javob berishni va har kuni oz-ozdan mashq qilishni oʻrgatadi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasi kundalik odat tilida: hozirgi oddiy zamon (present simple + 3-shaxs -s) va chastota ravishlari (always / usually / never).
intro.en: The first lesson is about the method itself. AJ Hoge teaches you to listen in a peak state, answer out loud, and practise a little every day. This week's two grammar topics are the language of daily habit: the present simple (+ third-person -s) and adverbs of frequency (always / usually / never).

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida oʻz kundalik tartibingizni toʻxtamasdan tasvirlab bera olasiz va biror ishni qanchalik tez-tez qilishingizni (always/usually/rarely) toʻgʻri soʻz tartibida ayta olasiz.
canDo.en: By the end you can describe your daily routine without pausing, and say how often you do something (always/usually/rarely) with correct word order.

grammar.titleUz: Hozirgi oddiy zamon: odat va tartib (+ 3-shaxs -s)
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 1 — talk about your routine and what you do
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can describe habits and routines accurately (he/she + -s)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida feʼl shaxsga qarab oʻzgaradi, lekin 3-shaxs birlik uchun alohida “-s” yoʻq. Ingliz tili esa he/she/it bilan feʼlga majburiy -s qoʻshadi: “U ishlaydi” = “She works” (❗ “She work” emas). Savol va inkorda esa do/does yordamchisi keladi va -s aynan does ichiga oʻtadi: “Does she work?”.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ She work in a bank. He don’t speak English. ✅ She works in a bank. He doesn’t speak English. He/she/it bilan feʼlga -s; savol/inkorda esa does/doesn’t (asosiy feʼl sof qoladi).

examples.en: I listen to English every day.
examples.uz: Men har kuni ingliz tili tinglayman.

exercises.hintUz: he/she/it uchun savol → Does; keyin feʼl sof qoladi (speak)

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): kundalik tartibingizdan uchta jumla — “I … every day. My day starts with … . It makes me … .”
exercises.hintUz: present simple; I/you/we/they bilan sof feʼl, he/she/it bilan -s

grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 1 — say how often you do things naturally
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can place always/usually/never in the right position
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekchada “doim”, “baʼzan” kabi soʻzlar jumlaning boshida ham, oxirida ham kelaveradi. Ingliz tilida chastota ravishi qatʼiy joyda turadi: oddiy feʼldan OLDIN (I always try), lekin be/yordamchi feʼldan KEYIN (I am always busy; I have never tried).
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I go always to bed late. I am never late usually. ✅ I always go to bed late. I am usually late. Ravish oddiy feʼldan oldin, “be” (am/is/are) dan keyin turadi.

examples.en: I usually wake up at six.
I have never been to London.

exercises.prompt: I ___ drink coffee in the morning. (usually)
exercises.hintUz: chastota ravishi oddiy feʼldan OLDIN: usually drink

exercises.prompt: ___ I feel nervous before I speak.
exercises.hintUz: sometimes/usually/often jumla boshida ham kela oladi (always/never emas)

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda beshta jumla ayting: “I always … I usually … I sometimes … I rarely … I never … .”
exercises.hintUz: ravishni oddiy feʼldan oldin, “be” dan keyin qoʻying

vocab.example: Grammar and vocabulary are linguistic factors.

vocab.uz: til bilan bogʻliq boʻlmagan omillar (his, holat)
vocab.defEn: things not about language, e.g. emotion and energy
vocab.example: Your emotional state is a non-linguistic factor.

vocab.defEn: the part of the mind below active thought
vocab.example: You want to engage your subconscious.

vocab.defEn: to turn on and use something
vocab.example: Engage your emotions when you study.

vocab.defEn: what is happening in your body
vocab.example: Strong physiology means standing up straight and moving.
Study in a peak emotional state.

vocab.uz: eng yuqori natija, choʻqqi darajadagi ish
vocab.defEn: doing something at your very best
the general feeling you have for a while
vocab.example: I try to keep a positive emotional state.
You can master English with daily practice.

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda ikki talaba sinfdagi turli millat vakillari haqida gaplashadi. Kirish darsining gʻoyasiga mos: ingliz tili — butun dunyoni bogʻlaydigan til. Sizningcha, koʻp millatli sinfda oʻqish foydalimi yoki qiyinmi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue two students talk about the many nationalities in their classes. It fits the intro theme: English is the language that connects the whole world. Do you think a multinational class is helpful or hard?

keyVocab.uz: Birlashgan Millatlar Tashkiloti (bu yerda: koʻp millat vakili)
keyVocab.defEn: an international political organization; here, a mix of many nationalities
a large group or variety of people or things

keyVocab.uz: til toʻsigʻi (til bilmaslikdan kelib chiqadigan qiyinchilik)
keyVocab.defEn: difficulty communicating because people speak different languages
to keep going until the end; to persevere
to understand or connect with someone
to become better than something or someone

sixmin.title: How do you learn to speak a language?

quiz.q: How many languages are there in the world altogether?
quiz.qUz: Dunyoda hammasi boʻlib nechta til bor?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: c) 7,000. Rob b) 700 dedi va xato qildi. Dunyoda taxminan 7,000 ta til bor, ammo UNESCO ularning 2,500 tasini yoʻqolib ketish xavfida deb belgilagan.

vocab.defEn: to learn something thoroughly or well
how often something happens or occurs
changes to the basic form of a word according to its function
the simplest, base form of a word before an inflection is added
the basics that your learning grows from

funEnglish.title: The secrets of learning a new language | Lýdia Machová | TED

speakingPrompt.en: Describe your typical day. What do you usually do in the morning, afternoon and evening? How often do you study English?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: hozirgi oddiy zamon (I get up…, she works…) va chastota ravishlari (always / usually / sometimes / never).
